Module 2:
Tools, Part 1
!
Executive Summary
Now we’re going to talk about some of the tools that we use in our
launches. These are tools that we use and recommend.
I’m going to cover launch pages, funnel pages, webpages, video pages,
and all those types of things. How do you actually build the launch that
everyone is going to see?
First of all, the great thing is that these tools just keep on getting better. If
you don't see something on this list it doesn't mean it doesn't work. It
just means that I don't have experience with it.
“Horses for courses” - this idea is that in horse racing certain horses are
better for certain courses, compared to other courses.
There are different horses for different courses, and there are different tools
that are better for different situations. Just because you see me using a
specific tool doesn't mean that’s the one that you should automatically be
using.
!
!
!

-- 1 of 2 --

Tools
1. Leadpages (http://www.sixinseven.com/leadpages) - I absolutely love
Leadpages. This is how we create pretty much all the pages for our
business right now. It's great for squeeze pages, sales pages, webinar
pages, webcast pages, thank you pages, etc. They keep on adding
more pages, and they’re always testing designs.
2. Kajabi (http://www.sixinseven.com/kajabi) - We’ve used Kajabi for more
than three years, they have a great service. The PLF portal that you're in
right now is built using Kajabi. It's amazing for online video courses.
3. OptimizePress (http://www.sixinseven.com/optimizepress) - I’ve never
used this one myself. My team might have used it for some pages, but I
know a lot people really like it. It's good for squeeze pages and there's
launch funnel pages too.
4. WordPress (https://wordpress.org/) - a great, free, comprehensive CMS
(Content Management System) for building websites. It's a great place
to start. That's what my blog at JeffWalker.com is built on.
!
